description: Undergraduate major names stored as text. Contains 50 distinct, non-null values across 50 records (0% missing). All values are unique, representing a complete categorical listing of academic majors in this dataset.

Starting median salary stored as currency-formatted text. All 50 records are non-null with 43 distinct values ranging from $34,000.00 to $74,300.00. Values are highly uniform, consistently using a leading dollar sign, comma thousands separator, and two decimal places.
recommended_actions: [STANDARDIZE] Convert currency-formatted strings to numeric type by removing the '$' symbol and comma thousands separator: '$55,900.00' → 55900.00. | [VALIDATE] Ensure all values follow the format: leading dollar sign, comma thousands separator, exactly two decimal digits — e.g., '$55,900.00'.

description: Mid-career median salary stored as text in currency format with a leading '$', comma thousands separators, and a '.00' decimal suffix. All 50 records are non-null and contain 49 distinct values, with '$52,000.00' appearing twice. Observed value lengths are 10–11 characters depending on magnitude. Stored as string, which enforces lexicographic ordering (e.g., '$101,000.00' is evaluated as less than '$98,600.00') and prevents numeric aggregation.
recommended_actions: [STANDARDIZE] Convert currency-formatted strings to numeric decimal by removing '$' and ',' characters: '$95,500.00' → 95500.00. Applies to 100% of records. | [VALIDATE] Ensure all values follow the format: a dollar sign, comma-separated thousands, and exactly two decimal places — e.g., '$95,500.00'.

Mid-career 75th percentile salary stored as currency-formatted text. All 50 records are non-null, with 44 distinct values. Every value follows a `$XX,XXX.00` or `$XXX,XXX.00` pattern. String storage causes lexicographic sorting anomalies: the reported minimum is `$100,000.00` and maximum is `$98,800.00`, whereas the actual observed range is `$70,500.00` to `$145,000.00`.
recommended_actions: [STANDARDIZE] Convert currency-formatted strings to numeric type by removing '$' and ',' characters: '$122,000.00' → 122000.00, '$88,000.00' → 88000.00. | [VALIDATE] Ensure all values match the currency text format before conversion: a leading '$', comma-separated thousands, and two decimal places — e.g., `$122,000.00`.
